title: Fort Sumter Start Point
summary: A solid shoreside choice if the crew wants history with harbor context.
display_text: Liberty Square is the main jumping-off point for Fort Sumter visits, and it gives a crew an easy way to swap dock lines for some genuine harbor history.
audio_text: If Charleston has you in a history mood, Liberty Square is the place to begin. It is the main departure point for Fort Sumter, which is a decent excuse to leave the boat for a while.
source_credit: Source: Charleston Area CVB listing for Fort Sumter Visitor Education Center at Liberty Square

title: Harbor Views Ashore
summary: Easy visual payoff for crews wanting a simple leg-stretcher.
display_text: Battery and White Point Gardens offer harbor views, Fort Sumter views, and the sort of easy walk that can improve the mood of any crew after a day aboard.
audio_text: If the crew needs a stretch, the Battery is a good Charleston answer. You get harbor views, a look toward Fort Sumter, and scenery that does not smell like diesel.
source_credit: Source: Charleston Area CVB listing for Historic Charleston Battery / Whitepoint Gardens
notes: Good fallback side card when user asks generally about Charleston.

title: Downtown, But Stay Awake
summary: Useful practical note for a popular downtown stop.
display_text: Charleston Maritime Center is praised for downtown access and walkability, but reports also note strong cross currents on entry and exposure to wake and swell.
audio_text: Charleston Maritime Center is handy for town, but do not let the location flatter you into relaxing too early. The cross current can make a casual arrival look needlessly theatrical.
source_credit: Source: Waterway Guide review summary for Charleston Maritime Center
notes: High-value practical tip for Charleston marina queries.

title: Useful Town Stop
summary: Compact practical services note for transient boaters.
display_text: Charleston Maritime Center is set up for transient use with dockage, power, fresh water, showers, laundry, and restaurants reachable on foot, though the walk is not especially short.
audio_text: For a practical stop, Charleston Maritime Center checks several boxes. Showers, laundry, and food nearby can make a crew feel civilized again.
source_credit: Source: CruisersNet marina listing for Charleston Maritime Center
notes: Pairs well with marina answer flows.

title: Provisioning Made Easier
summary: A practical cruiser note emphasizing shoreside access.
display_text: One repeatedly praised advantage at St. Johns Yacht Harbor is the courtesy car, which makes provisioning and exploring the Charleston area materially easier.
audio_text: A courtesy car can turn a marina from pleasant to strategic. At St. Johns Yacht Harbor, that little detail helps with both provisioning runs and crew morale.
source_credit: Source: Waterway Guide review summary for St. Johns Yacht Harbor
notes: Strong operational tip when Charleston query includes provisioning.

title: ICW Pace Reminder
summary: General ICW framing note adapted for sidebar use.
display_text: The ICW rewards crews who stay prepared and flexible. Charleston is a good example of a stop where practical planning and a little patience usually pay off.
audio_text: The ICW is rewarding, but it likes a prepared crew. Charleston is a good place to remember that the best pace is usually steady, not heroic.
source_credit: Tip adapted from The Boat Galley / Carolyn Shearlock
notes: Keep explicit Carolyn / Boat Galley credit.

title: Sleep Better at Anchor
display_text: A dependable anchor setup is cheaper than a long, anxious night listening for trouble.
audio_text: A good anchor is a lot quieter than wondering at two in the morning whether you are still where you left yourself.

title: More Water, Less Fuss
display_text: A reliable watermaker can make long cruising stretches more comfortable and less dependent on the next dock.
audio_text: Fresh water aboard has a way of improving both comfort and diplomacy. It is hard to overstate the value of that.
